Barack Obama:
Barack Obama (Barack Obama) is an American politician, writer and leader, who served as the 44th President of America from 2009 to 2017. His biography is as follows:

Birth and early life
Birth: 4 April 1961 in the city of Ohio of Hawaii. His father, Barack Obama small, was a Kenyan politician, while mother, Mary Obama, was an American American.
Educational qualification: He obtained a bachelor degree in political science from Oxford University (California), then obtained legal education from Columbia University (New York). He studied in the law department of Harvard University and worked as an editor of the law university.
Beginning of political career
Social worker: In the 1980s, he worked as a social worker in Chicago, where he worked on issues of poverty, education and employment.
State member of Illinois: In 1996, he contested election as a state member of Illinois and in 2004 he worked as a member of the state house. He is known for his conversation and political leadership.
As President of America
First election (2008): Obama won the 2008 presidential election under the leadership of the Democratic Party, which was the first time in the history of America an African-American president was elected.
Second election (2012): He won his second election in 2012, in which he was elected twice as President of America.
Main political achievements
Health policy (Affordable Care Act): In 2010, he worked as President of America for a new law for America’s health policy, which was called "Obama Care Act".
International relations: He is known for the Iran nuclear agreement (2015) and for the leadership of America in development programs in Africa.

Dilma Rousseff:
Biography of Dilma Rousseff
(step-by-step description)

1. Birth and Educational Life

Birth: 13 January 1947, in the state of Minas Gerais of Brazil.

Family: Her father was a teacher, while mother was a teacher.

Education: She studied in the field of science and engineering. She obtained a degree in science and engineering from the Bolshevist University of Brazil (UFSCAR), and later obtained a master’s degree in university studies at the Bolshevist University of Brazil (USP).

2. Political Beginning

In the 1980s: She started politics by joining the Workers’ Party (PT) of Brazil.

In 1989: She contested elections as a member of the state Bolshevist State Assembly of Brazil.

In the 1990s: She worked in various state government jobs in the field of education and development.

3. Urban Leadership and National Politics

In 2001: She won election as mayor of São Paulo city. During her governance many initiatives for city development and social reform were taken.

In 2002: She won election as a member of parliament of Brazil.

In 2010: She won election as President of Brazil, who became the first female president of Brazil.

4. Presidential Term (2011-2016)

Main policies:

Economic policy: She tried to reduce the budget under a savings policy during the economic crisis.

Social program: She promoted the "Bolsa Família" program, which is an important plan for poverty and education.
